message:
fix: Require Alt modifier for vim-style navigation shortcuts

Plain letter keys (h/j/k/l/g/G) were intercepting characters before
they reached query input, making searches impossible for words
containing those letters (e.g., "soldier" would show "sodier").

All vim-style navigation now requires Alt modifier:
- Alt+j/k: Move down/up in results or scroll detail
- Alt+h/l: Move left/right between panes
- Alt+g/G: Jump to first/last item

Arrow keys continue to work without modifiers.

Also ensured Ctrl+M and Ctrl+Y (multi-select and yank) require their
modifiers as documented.

Principle: All shortcuts that could conflict with text input MUST
use special keys (F-keys, Home, End, etc.) or require Ctrl/Alt modifiers.

Bumps version to 0.1.30.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 description = "Unified TUI search over local coding agent histories"
